meta.title: Greenways content modules
meta.description: Independent HTML/video illustrations agents open in the shared modal shell — each row includes what-it-is (description) and how-to-use (usageHint).

modules.usageHint: Filter by sector or topic, jump to a city, and click markers for case studies and verified savings examples.

media: 514 case studies and 391 directory organisations on an interactive map — filter by sector, city, or topic.
Click markers for verified savings stats and techniques to benchmark before you plan upgrades.
Use when you need real-world examples for stakeholders, not just policy headlines.
products: Add New Company.
equipment: Add New Company.

modules.usageHint: Pick your building type, follow the journey steps, and note timing before you apply for grants or finance.

equipment: 🌍 Eco Project Planning Guide.

modules.title: Energy prices ticker
modules.description: Live wholesale €/MWh lanes — same feed as the Greenways buildings dashboard.
modules.usageHint: Read the moving price bands for context, then pair with tariff compare or a savings projection before you finance an upgrade.

finance: Live wholesale €/MWh lanes — same feed as the Greenways buildings dashboard — for timing upgrades and tariff reviews.
Retail bills can lag wholesale by weeks; use this as an early signal, not your contract rate.
Pair with savings trajectory when usage and unit costs both trend up.
media: Wholesale €/MWh lanes give context for sustainability headlines — price moves explain why policy stories hit operator budgets.
Same feed as the buildings dashboard; cite when news items mention volatile energy markets.
Pair with the sustainability map when a story needs a site-level example, not just spot prices.
deals: Live wholesale €/MWh lanes — same feed as the Greenways buildings dashboard.
Read the moving price bands for context, then pair with tariff compare or a savings projection before you finance an upgrade.

default.label: Why watch wholesale prices?
default.body: Wholesale €/MWh moves eventually flow into retail energy bills. When unit costs rise, the same site spends more even if usage stays flat. Pair this ticker with a savings trajectory so you can plan upgrades and tariff reviews before the next price jump.

finance.label: Vincent — prices, bills, and planning ahead
finance.body: Wholesale €/MWh is the early signal for what restaurants and sites pay on supply contracts. When lanes move up, every inefficient hour on kitchens, HVAC, or refrigeration costs more — even before your tariff formally renews. I use this ticker with clients to time ETL upgrades, BNPL, and green loans. Open the savings trajectory next: it shows where usage is heading so you can counter price rises instead of reacting after the bill lands.

media.label: Cheryce — context for sustainability news
media.body: Energy headlines make more sense when you can see wholesale lanes moving in real time. Price shifts explain why policy and market stories eventually hit operator budgets — not just emissions targets. I pair the ticker with case studies on the map and the savings trajectory when a site needs a forward-looking story, not just today's spot price.

grants.label: Andrieus — fund before the bill spike
grants.body: When wholesale prices climb, grants and subsidies matter more because they shrink the net capex on efficient equipment. Watch the ticker to spot when bills are under pressure, then stack non-repayable support with verified ETL products. The savings trajectory helps you show assessors a planned path — not a panic upgrade after costs jump.

equipment.label: Artemis — upgrade timing vs unit costs
equipment.body: Rising €/MWh magnifies every inefficient oven, fridge, and HVAC hour. The ticker tells you when running old equipment is getting expensive; the trajectory shows whether usage trends make an upgrade urgent. Model payback in savings projection before you pick alternatives in the deep dive.

deals.label: Zara — tariffs when markets move
deals.body: Wholesale lanes move before retail packages reprice — useful when you're comparing supplier deals or renewal windows. Even on a better tariff, efficient equipment lowers the kWh you still buy. Watch the ticker for timing, then open the tariff portal or trajectory to plan the switch alongside upgrades.

products.label: Zyanne — efficient products when prices bite
products.body: Higher wholesale prices mean verified efficient appliances pay back faster because each saved kWh is worth more. Use the ticker for market context, then shortlist ETL-listed options in the product finder and check trajectory to see if usage trends justify acting now.

systems.label: Edwardo — measure, then plan
systems.body: You cannot manage what you do not meter — but wholesale prices tell you why metering urgency changes month to month. When lanes rise, baseline visibility and trajectory planning help you prioritise sensors, audits, and upgrades before bills run away.

modules.title: Utility detail
modules.description: Site-level electricity, gas, or water view with usage breakdown and drill-down charts.
modules.usageHint: Choose the utility type, scan KPIs and trends, then use the view to spot where an upgrade or tariff review helps most.

modules.title: European energy deals
modules.description: Compare business and site tariffs across European markets in one portal.
modules.usageHint: Set your market or building type, compare headline rates, then open a deal lane that matches your contract renewal timing.

finance: Headline business and site tariffs across NL, UK, DE, FR, and other EU markets — useful at contract renewal, not only when wholesale spikes.
Pair tariff rows with the energy prices ticker: retail packages can lag wholesale moves by weeks or months.
Even on a better tariff, efficient equipment lowers the kWh you still buy — stack supply switching with ETL upgrades.
deals: European Energy Deals Portal — compare tariffs and curated Greenways routes.

finance.label: Vincent — retail supply vs wholesale
finance.body: Wholesale €/MWh is the early signal; this portal is where you compare what you actually sign. I use it with clients before renewal windows close, then model whether upgrades shrink the kWh left on the bill.

modules.title: Finance finder
modules.description: Restaurant finance hub — grants, BNPL, equipment finance, and green loans in one place.
modules.usageHint: Open the tab that matches your need (grant, pay-later, lease, or green loan), read the example tile, then stack with a savings projection.

finance: Five lanes in one hub: grants, BNPL, equipment finance, green loans, and Europe-wide programmes — each with example tiles and hints.
Best used after payback looks credible: open savings projection or the Product Calculator first, then pick the tab that matches capex size.
Grants tab links non-repayable support; equipment finance and BNPL cover kitchen and HVAC when subsidies do not cover the full swap.
grants: Grants tab surfaces non-repayable hospitality and equipment subsidies — cross-check rows against the schemes portals for deadlines.
When a grant covers part of capex, Vincent's equipment finance and BNPL tabs handle the remainder.
Example tiles show typical stack paths for restaurant upgrades in your region profile.

finance.label: Vincent — stack funding paths
finance.body: I route clients here once the upgrade case is clear — grants shrink net capex, BNPL and equipment finance spread kitchen swaps, green loans suit building-scale retrofits. Andrieus owns scheme detail; I own how you pay for verified efficient equipment.

modules.title: Restaurant schemes portal
modules.description: Browse hospitality and restaurant grants and subsidies in one catalogue — deadlines, regions, and official links.
modules.usageHint: Filter by region or topic, open scheme rows for eligibility notes, then select two schemes in chat to compare side by side.

grants: Hospitality and restaurant grants in one catalogue — filter by region, topic, and deadline before you shortlist schemes in chat.
Each row links to official programme detail; select two schemes here or in chat to compare side by side.
Pair with finance finder when non-repayable support does not cover full equipment capex.

grants.label: Andrieus — restaurant schemes first
grants.body: This is the home catalogue for NL, UK, and EU hospitality subsidies — filter to your profile region, then compare rows in chat when two programmes look close.

modules.title: EU schemes portal
modules.description: EU-wide programmes and cross-border funding — Horizon Europe, LIFE, and regional overlays in one browse view.
modules.usageHint: Set your sector context, scan EU-wide rows, then pair with the restaurant portal or finance finder when you need stacked funding paths.

grants: EU-wide programmes — Horizon Europe, LIFE, and cross-border overlays — in one browse view with sector filters.
Stack EU rows with the restaurant portal when hospitality schemes and EU innovation funding both apply.
Official detail links on each row; use chat compare when two EU programmes overlap your upgrade.

modules.title: Savings projection
modules.description: Payback chart — do nothing vs upgrade, with grants and illustrative tax overlays.
modules.usageHint: Enter or accept the scenario values, compare the curves, then use the result to decide whether to finance now or wait.

equipment: Charts do-nothing vs upgrade curves with grant chips and illustrative tax bands — capex bands for single equipment (~€25k) and whole-building (~€150k).
Opens from equipment deep dive alternative cards so payback stays tied to the spec you are comparing.
Use before handoff to Vincent: a credible curve makes BNPL, equipment finance, or green loans easier to justify.
finance: Same payback chart Artemis uses — do nothing vs upgrade with grants and tax overlays so you finance against a visible break-even.
Fridge demo scenario gives a worked example when you want numbers before entering your own capex.
Pair with finance finder tabs once the upgrade curve crosses your comfort threshold.

modules.title: Energy Technology List (official)
modules.description: UK government-backed ETL register — browse the scheme in their own words before you shortlist on Greenways.
modules.usageHint: Read how ETL verifies products (top 25% in class), then open the official site for categories and product search — Greenways etl_* rows use the same register.

equipment: Opens the official UK Energy Technology List overview — independently verified products, typically top quartile in class.
Use when stakeholders need DESNZ-backed wording before you compare etl_* alternatives in deep dive or marketplace.
Official site links open in a new tab; this module keeps Artemis aligned with the scheme operators.
finance: Ground procurement conversations in the government register before Vincent models payback on a specific etl_* pick.
Official verification reduces equipment-finance risk — lenders like seeing top-quartile efficiency evidence.
Pair with Product Calculator once you have a shortlist from the register.
products: Same verified register behind Greenways catalog rows — read ETL category rules on the official site first.
Product search on etl.energysecurity.gov.uk complements our sustainable-product and ETL finder lanes.
Keeps claims aligned with the scheme operators rather than paraphrase alone.

equipment.label: Artemis — official ETL alignment
equipment.body: Start here when anyone asks what ETL is or wants the scheme’s own explanation; follow with ETL finder for Greenways shortlists.

modules.title: ETL product finder
modules.description: Verified efficient equipment catalog — search ETL-listed products with grant overlays.
modules.usageHint: Search by category or kitchen asset, open a product card, then jump to marketplace detail or savings projection from the match.

equipment: Search verified ETL-listed kitchen and HVAC products with grant overlays matched to your region profile.
Each match links to marketplace detail (`etl_*` ids) and can feed the deep dive decision matrix.
Use when you know the asset class (combi, wok, fridge) but need a shortlist before comparing specs side by side.
finance: ETL-listed products carry verified savings data — the business case Vincent needs before equipment finance or BNPL.
Grant overlays on each row show non-repayable support you can stack with green loans or leases.
Open the Product Calculator next to compare energy use vs your current model before signing finance.
products: Verified efficient equipment catalog — search ETL-listed products with grant overlays.
Search by category or kitchen asset, open a product card, then jump to marketplace detail or savings projection from the match.

equipment.label: Artemis — verified shortlist
equipment.body: Start here when you need ETL-backed options fast; follow with deep dive for side-by-side alternatives and savings projection for payback.

modules.title: Sustainable renovations

modules.title: Insulation guide

Stack with Andrieus for fabric grants and Vincent when payback justifies green loans.

Pick a plan shape (quick wins vs full retrofit) then jump to ETL finder or deep dive for each equipment lane.

modules.usageHint: Scan the trend lines, compare days, and use the pattern to time upgrades or tariff reviews.

Pair with the energy prices ticker: rising wholesale plus rising usage is the signal to finance upgrades early.
Use before renewal conversations to show assessors a forward path, not a reactive bill spike.

modules.description: Bill breakdown guide — where restaurant and site energy spend goes.
modules.usageHint: Follow the sections for your utility type, note the biggest cost drivers, then open projection or finance tools.

Use when the bill feels high but the team cannot name which loads to target first.

modules.usageHint: Enter current equipment and usage, compare product options (including ETL-listed rows today; more catalogues coming), then use savings figures in finance or grants conversations.

finance: Compare energy use of efficient products vs what you run today — ETL-listed rows are the primary input lane today.
Outputs savings figures you can carry into finance finder tabs or grants conversations with Andrieus.
Best after you have a product shortlist from ETL finder; best before you sign equipment finance.

finance.body: I use the Product Calculator to ground BNPL and lease conversations — verified efficient equipment should show lower kWh before you commit to monthly payments.
